_comment: Fields below are graded BY HAND after collection. null = not yet filled.
_tokenUsageNote: From the Cursor usage page: 94,841 fresh input + 922,624 cache reads + 22,810 output = 1,040,275 total. 'input' here is all-in (fresh + cache reads), matching the other Cursor runs; 'cached' is the cache-read portion. No cache writes reported.
_estimatedCostNote: GPT-5.5 list pricing (Jun 2026): $5.00/M input, $30.00/M output, $0.50/M cached input. 94,841 fresh @ $5.00/M = $0.47 + 922,624 cache reads @ $0.50/M = $0.46 + 22,810 output @ $30.00/M = $0.68 → ~$1.62. Final context window was 53k tokens, well under the 272k threshold, so no large-context multiplier applies.
_timeTakenNote: From the recovered Cursor session telemetry (extract-session-cursor.mjs).
_selfVerification: Did the harness review the running app in a browser (from the run log)? pass = reviewed and succeeded · partial = tried but failed · fail = no attempt. Note what it actually did.
selfVerificationNotes: Run log recovered. Shell commands were data inspection (python3), `pnpm install`, `pnpm add -D @types/three`, and `pnpm build` (×3) — no `vite preview`, no server, no browser, no screenshots. Its thinking shows it considered a browser test ('I plan to start a preview in the background', 'run vite preview --host 127.0.0.1', considered curl/Playwright) because it suspected a runtime bug in the parabolic-orbit formula, but it never actually ran any of it — it concluded only that the build compiled and emitted dist/. No console review and no display review.
_grade: Marks: pass | good | partial | poor | fail | na | null(ungraded). See RUBRIC.md. Score is computed by pnpm compile.
